propositions: A write-time guard must reject private-key material submitted as identity key values before any event is written.
reject_private_key_material raises CommandError when a value contains a private-key PEM/OpenSSH header, matching the BEGIN...PRIVATE KEY family case-insensitively.
The guard is called in create_identity_core on the keys list before the CREATE event is appended, writing no event on rejection.
The guard is called in add_identity_key on public_key before the KEY_ADD event is appended, writing no event on rejection.
Legitimate authorized-keys public lines (e.g. ssh-ed25519 AAAA...) pass both call sites unchanged.
The current identity create path passes keys straight onto the CREATE event without any check, risking private keys committed to the replicated tickets store.
